signals.reasoning: BTC remains trend-supported and near fresh highs, but the live Fed-shock veto blocks chasing a new breakout entry.
ETH has strong recovery structure, but the event brake outweighs upgrading it at these levels.
SOL is back in strong rebound mode, but it is already extended and should stay watch-only under the live veto.
BOME is too stretched and too headline-sensitive to justify a fresh signal here.
MERL is strong and trend-supported, but the rebound is already too advanced to chase through a veto board.
PNUT is constructive, but it is still just a rebound continuation candidate and not clean enough to override the event brake.
ORDI has some squeeze support and solid price action, but the live veto is enough to keep it watch-only instead of actionable.
WIF is recovering but not with enough differentiation to justify forcing a new signal through a veto.
TST is still too extended and too crowded to trust as a disciplined entry.
KNEIRO has incomplete market data in the request snapshot, so it should remain off-limits even though the raw squeeze setup looks interesting.
